questions.question: What is the time complexity of Insertion Sort when the input array is already sorted?
questions.explanation: Insertion Sort has a time complexity of O(n) when the input array is already sorted.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of Insertion Sort?
questions.explanation: Insertion Sort is a stable sorting algorithm.

questions.question: What is the best-case time complexity of Insertion Sort?
questions.explanation: The best-case time complexity of Insertion Sort is O(n) when the input array is sorted.

questions.question: How many comparisons are made in the worst-case scenario of Insertion Sort?
questions.explanation: In the worst-case scenario, Insertion Sort makes 3N comparisons.

questions.question: What is the space complexity of Insertion Sort?
questions.explanation: Insertion Sort has a space complexity of O(1) since it only uses a constant amount of extra memory.
